claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions. Let me start with the first one:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'm asking about. His name is Alex and he's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's good to know. Let me continue with the next question:

**Are you responsible for Alex? This means you're looking after him and paying towards things like food, clothing, or pocket money.**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for Alex. I look after him and pay for all his needs like food, clothing, and everything else he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Now I need to ask about your residency status:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to understand your immigration status:

**What is your immigration status in the UK? For example, are you a British citizen, do you have settled status, indefinite leave to remain, or are you subject to immigration control?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me just gather one more piece of information to complete the assessment:

**Is anyone else claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or is there anyone else who Alex lives with who might be claiming for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for answering all my questions. Before I provide my final assessment, let me read the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the information you've provided and the official guidance, I can now provide you with my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- He lives with you
- You are responsible for him and pay for his needs
- You live in the UK and are a British citizen
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. You can proceed with making a claim if you haven't already done so.

Please note: If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You would still receive the Child Benefit, but may need to pay some or all of it back through your tax return.

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. He lives with you, and you are responsible for him by paying for his food, clothing, and other needs. You live in the UK and are a British citizen, which satisfies the residency and immigration requirements. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible if you live with them. All of these conditions are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK with one child, Alex, who is 8 years old. The claimant lives with Alex, is responsible for him, pays for all his needs, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met.

You live in the UK and are a British citizen, which satisfies the residency and immigration requirements. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible if you live with them. All of these conditions are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK with one child, Alex, who is 8 years old. The claimant lives with Alex, is responsible for him, pays for all his needs, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met.
